INDIA.title: Track 1 — The GSO Stalker: First Contact
INDIA.text: ISRO SSA Network at Pune flags an anomaly: PAKSAT-1R has quietly exited its ITU-assigned slot at 38E and is executing a series of low-delta-V burns. Current range to GSAT-7A (Indian Navy primary fleet comms satellite): 80km. Relative velocity: near-zero, indicating a co-orbital shadow maneuver.
INDIA.notes: GSO slots are assigned by the ITU under RR Article 44. Unauthorized slot deviation is a Treaty violation. However, 'stalking' (shadowing at safe range) is a legal grey area. The Chinese Shijian-21 satellite demonstrated this in 2022 when it towed a dead satellite to graveyard orbit. GSAT-7A handles Indian Navy VSAT, maritime patrol aircraft datalinks, and Andaman chain comms — losing it creates a strategic blindspot in the Indian Ocean.

options.label: Lodge UN-COPUOS Protest
options.log: Formal protest lodged. The clock is ticking.

options.label: Proactive Maneuver — Offset GSAT-7A
options.log: GSAT-7A relocated to safe-haven orbital slot.

options.label: Broadcast Warning on ITU Emergency Frequency
options.log: Public warning broadcast; international monitoring activated.

options.label: Kinetic Intercept (ASAT)
options.log: Target destroyed. 5,000 fragments in GEO corridor.

INDIA.title: Track 1 — Diplomatic Path: Ignored
INDIA.text: 48 hours later: the protest was filed, acknowledged, and quietly buried. OPFOR has now closed to 10km, matching GSAT-7A velocity precisely. Thermal imaging reveals a mechanical arm deployment — a 'Sticky Finger' mechanism capable of physically grappling a satellite.
INDIA.notes: Proximity RPO at under 10km is increasingly common. Russia's Cosmos-2542 performed this on a US spy satellite in 2020. The robotic arm is dual-use: legally a refueling/repair mechanism; militarily it can grab, spin, or tow an asset into an uncontrolled trajectory. India has no legal recourse — no treaty prohibits proximity inspection unless contact occurs.

options.label: Active Dazzle — Flash OPFOR Optics
options.log: OPFOR optics temporarily saturated. Arm retracted.

options.label: Electronic Shielding + Uplink Hardening
options.log: GSAT-7A uplink hardened; spoofing attempts blocked.

options.label: Alert US Space Command + Request Shared Watch
options.log: US Space Command tracking OPFOR; SIGINT shared.

INDIA.title: Track 1 — Tactical Path: OPFOR Matches Burn
INDIA.text: Your relocation burn moved GSAT-7A 150km eastward. Within 6 hours, OPFOR matched the burn exactly, closing to 40km. They have deployed a sub-satellite — a 3U CubeSat inspector — into a trailing orbit behind GSAT-7A blind spot, using optical and RF sensors to map your satellite bus architecture.
INDIA.notes: Co-orbital inspection via sub-satellite was pioneered by Russia's Olymp (Luch) satellite family. The inspector CubeSat orbits in the 'cone of silence' — directly behind the host satellite where its antenna patterns have blind coverage. Mapping a satellite bus architecture allows an adversary to identify solar panel hinge points, attitude thrusters, and antenna ports — precisely the data needed to design a targeted jamming or grappling attack.

options.label: Release Counter-Inspector CubeSat
options.log: RPO standoff initiated. OPFOR inspector neutralized.

options.label: Narrow-Beam LOS Comms Only
options.log: Signal footprint reduced to line-of-sight beams only.

options.label: Execute Fast Drift — Change Inclination +0.5 degrees
options.log: Inclination change shakes trailing inspector.

INDIA.title: Track 1 — Warning Path: OPFOR Escalates
INDIA.text: The warning broadcast trended on social media. OPFOR issued a counter-statement claiming their satellite is performing 'routine orbital maintenance' and accused India of 'militarizing civilian space assets.' Their satellite is now 20km away and accelerating.
INDIA.notes: Information operations in the space domain are as important as the physical maneuvers. Russia's TASS and China's Xinhua maintain dedicated space correspondents to quickly frame military space activities as civilian or defensive. India's lack of a dedicated space public affairs bureau means OPFOR narratives consistently dominate first-cycle media reporting.

options.label: Release SSA Data Publicly — Counter Narrative
options.log: Raw tracking data declassified; international support grows.

options.label: Emergency GEO Relocation
options.log: GSAT-7A moved out of OPFOR operational envelope.

INDIA.title: Track 1 — Standoff: The Dual-Use Dilemma
INDIA.text: OPFOR has deployed a second arm attempting to latch onto GSAT-7A solar panel frame. Your counter-inspector is 30 meters away. One burn could ram it into the OPFOR asset — but a collision at GEO altitude (35,786km) creates debris that will NOT naturally decay for centuries.
INDIA.notes: GEO debris is permanent. Unlike LEO debris that re-enters within years, a collision at GEO creates fragments remaining in the 35,786km belt essentially forever. The entire GEO arc — humanity's prime real estate for communications, weather, and navigation — could be rendered unusable. The 1972 Liability Convention holds the launching state responsible for all damage caused by its space objects.

options.label: Ram Counter-Inspector into OPFOR Arm (Kinetic)
options.log: OPFOR arm destroyed; GEO debris event triggered.

options.label: EMP Pulse from Counter-Inspector (Non-Kinetic)
options.log: OPFOR electronics reset; arm retracted. India maintains high ground.

options.label: Stand Down — Accept Inspection Under Protest
options.log: Political cost accepted; asset integrity preserved.

INDIA.title: Track 1 — Electronic Hardening: Uplink Under Fire
INDIA.text: With GSAT-7A uplink hardened, OPFOR shifts tactics. They broadcast spurious telecommand packets on GSAT-7A public TT&C frequency. While your hardening blocks most attempts, three packets slip through — your Attitude Control System makes small unauthorized thruster burns, slowly pushing you out of your station-keeping box.
INDIA.notes: TT&C frequencies are published in the ITU Master International Frequency Register — technically public. Attackers exploit this by crafting valid-format but unauthorized commands. The GSAT-7 series uses S-band TT&C (2025-2110 MHz), shared with many legacy systems and harder to encrypt without a full bus redesign.

options.label: Transition to Quantum-Key Encrypted Uplink
options.log: QKD uplink active; all spurious packets cryptographically rejected.

options.label: Physical Switch to Backup Encrypted Transponder
options.log: Backup transponder online; unauthorized commands blocked.

INDIA.title: Track 1 — US Coalition: Three-Satellite Pincer
INDIA.text: US Space Command shares full sensor coverage revealing: two additional OPFOR satellites are simultaneously repositioning toward India's RISAT-2BR1 and CARTOSAT-3. This is a coordinated three-satellite pincer — a planned blinding of India's entire military ISR constellation.
INDIA.notes: India's ISR constellation is small but highly capable. RISAT-2BR1 uses an Israeli-designed X-band SAR that can see through clouds. CARTOSAT-3 has 0.25m resolution. Simultaneously blinding both would leave India's armed forces with zero real-time satellite imagery during a potential surface crisis — the textbook 'Space Pearl Harbor' scenario war-gamers have warned about since 2015.

options.label: Emergency Repositioning of All Three Assets
options.log: Full ISR constellation dispersed. OPFOR three-pronged attack defeated.

options.label: Activate Ground-Based UAV ISR Redundancy
options.log: UAV-based ISR activated as stopgap. Space assets in passive mode.

INDIA.title: CRISIS: GEO Debris Belt Created
INDIA.text: The collision at 35,786km altitude created approximately 800 fragments larger than 10cm. GEO debris does not decay — these fragments orbit for centuries, creating a minefield in the most commercially critical orbital band. 14 commercial satellite operators have filed emergency avoidance notices. ITU has convened an emergency session.
INDIA.notes: A GEO debris event is categorically different from LEO debris. No treaty prohibits kinetic action in orbit. An India-created GEO debris field would result in diplomatic isolation, ITU sanctions, and potential loss of India's future orbital slot allocations. The 2022 US unilateral ban on destructive ASAT testing was a first step but non-binding.

options.label: Accept Full International Liability + Fund Remediation
options.log: India accepts $4.2B liability claim. Diplomatic standing severely damaged.

options.label: Deploy Electrodynamic Tether to Shepherd Fragments
options.log: Experimental debris remediation activated. Partial success.

options.label: Deny Responsibility — Blame OPFOR Engineering Fault
options.log: Deniability attempted. International observers unconvinced.

INDIA.title: CRITICAL: LEO Debris — Kessler in Progress
INDIA.text: The ASAT strike produced 5,200 trackable fragments (>10cm) and an estimated 50,000 fragments >1cm. Twelve of India's operational LEO satellites are in the primary debris corridor. Impact probability per orbit: 8%.
INDIA.notes: The 2007 Chinese ASAT test on Fengyun-1C at 865km altitude is the single most consequential debris-generating event in history, responsible for approximately 3,000 tracked fragments still in orbit 17 years later. The Kessler Syndrome (1978) describes a cascade where collision debris creates more collisions. India's own space program would be the primary victim of any Indian ASAT strike at LEO inclinations where its own constellation resides.

options.label: Emergency Deorbit of Lowest-Priority Satellite
options.log: One asset sacrificed to clear corridor. Others survive.

options.label: Attempt Magnetic Shield + Orbit Raise
options.log: Experimental maneuver partially successful.

options.label: Emergency ISR Handoff to US/EU Partners
options.log: Partner assets provide bridge coverage. Strategic capability degraded 60%.

INDIA.title: Track 2 — Ground Segment Breach: MCF Penetration
INDIA.text: The Bengaluru Master Control Facility reports a Level 3 physical security breach. CCTV shows two individuals in contractor uniforms entering the primary server room during a 03:15 shift change, exploiting a 4-minute gap in biometric logging when the backup generator activated. They had 9 minutes of unsupervised access to the command terminal rack.
INDIA.notes: The MCF at Hassan (Karnataka) controls India's entire GEO fleet. Physical access to a command terminal allows insertion of a hardware implant — a device smaller than a USB drive that can send pre-programmed telecommands on a timer, even after the intruders leave. The 2008 Landsat-7 and Terra satellite incident showed that mission network compromise is the fastest path to orbital asset control.

options.label: Immediate Lockdown — All Uplinks Suspended
options.log: All uplinks paused. Fleet in autonomous safe-mode.

options.label: Trace Implant — Deploy Hardware Forensics Team
options.log: Implant found in Rack C-7. Attribution confirmed: proxy cell linked to OPFOR signals intelligence.

options.label: Controlled Bleed — Feed False Telemetry to Implant
options.log: Double-agent operation activated. OPFOR receives false orbital data.

INDIA.title: Track 2 — Ransomware: Fleet Goes Dark
INDIA.text: The hardware implant activated 2 minutes before discovery. A dormant ransomware payload — 'VORTEX-7,' a known state-sponsored tool — has encrypted mission databases of three GSAT and one RISAT satellite. Their telemetry streams show all-zeros. The spacecraft are flying blind.
INDIA.notes: Satellite ransomware is not science fiction. In 2019, ESA confirmed it had thwarted a cyberattack on ground segment networks. 'Bricking' a satellite by overwriting its EEPROM is irreversible without a hardware-level backup frequency. ISRO's newer satellites carry a hard-wired backup transponder on a classified frequency that can accept a full software reset. Older assets do not have this feature.

options.label: Broadcast Hard-Reset via Classified Backup Frequency
options.log: Fleet rebooted via backup command channel. 3 of 4 satellites recovered.

options.label: Isolate Infected Nodes — Triage Unaffected Assets
options.log: Partial telemetry restored. Infected satellites in safe-hold.

options.label: Negotiate Decryption Key via Proxy
options.log: Key received. One satellite recovered. Political cost: severe.

INDIA.title: Track 2 — Forensics: Three-Hop Attribution Chain
INDIA.text: The hardware implant is a custom FPGA device with a GSM modem — it receives trigger commands via commercial cellular network, invisible to space-link monitoring. Forensic analysis of the SIM card reveals calls routed through a Pakistan-based number, then a UAE relay, to an IP address in Belarus. Three hops deep and legally deniable.
INDIA.notes: False-flag attribution is the hallmark of modern state-sponsored cyber operations. The SolarWinds attack (2020) used 14 layers of obfuscation; operators were not officially attributed for 10 months. In the space domain, attribution is even harder because satellite TT&C attacks leave no forensic artifacts on the satellite itself — only on ground segment hardware.

options.label: Share Attribution Data with Five Eyes Partners
options.log: Intelligence shared. Coalition condemnation issued. Sanctions proposed.

options.label: Offensive Cyber — Mirror Attack on OPFOR Ground Segment
options.log: OPFOR satellite control center hit. Their comms degraded 40%.

options.label: Publicize Implant Details + Technical Evidence
options.log: Global media covers attack. OPFOR diplomatically isolated.

INDIA.title: Track 2 — Counterintel: Feeding the Mole
INDIA.text: For 72 hours, India feeds deliberately incorrect orbital elements to the OPFOR implant. OPFOR's space operations center plans three intercept maneuvers based on false data — sending their inspector satellites to empty orbits. Their fuel reserves are now critically low.
INDIA.notes: Deception operations in the space domain are rare but historically decisive. During the Cold War, the US leaked false specifications of KH-9 reconnaissance satellites to mislead Soviet ASAT targeting. An enemy with incorrect ephemeris data wastes limited delta-V (fuel) on pointless maneuvers — a bloodless, deniable form of space denial.

options.label: Extend Deception — Feed Increasingly Erratic Data
options.log: OPFOR inspector satellites now fuel-critical and repositioned to useless orbits.

options.label: Terminate Deception + Expose Implant Publicly
options.log: Diplomatic win secured; deception advantages exhausted.

INDIA.title: Track 3 — Lunar South Pole: Heritage Site Standoff
INDIA.text: Chandrayaan-4 surface rover detects a foreign wheeled vehicle approaching the Vikram lander heritage site at 220 meters/hour. The foreign rover has a robotic excavation arm. Under the Artemis Accords (which India signed), designated heritage zones require safety distances — but the OPFOR nation has NOT signed the Accords.
INDIA.notes: The Artemis Accords (2020+) are bilateral — nations must individually sign. As of 2024, 43 nations have signed, but China and Russia have not. The 1967 Outer Space Treaty prohibits national appropriation of celestial bodies but says nothing about surface access restrictions. The Vikram lander site at 70.9S, 22.8E is adjacent to the highest concentration of water-ice detected by Chandrayaan-1.

options.label: Declare 2km Safety Zone — Artemis Accords Basis
options.log: Zone declared. Non-signatory refuses to acknowledge. International legal battle begins.

options.label: Deploy High-Power Laser Warning Flash
options.log: Foreign rover camera optics temporarily saturated. Forward movement halted.

options.label: Dispatch Chandrayaan Rover to Block Path
options.log: Your rover positioned between OPFOR and heritage site. Standoff at 12 meters.

options.label: Re-task Orbiter to Image Rover in 4K — Evidence Gathering
options.log: Full HD imagery of OPFOR rover transmitted to UN and international media.

INDIA.title: Track 3 — Lunar Resource Claim: Ice Extraction Begins
INDIA.text: The foreign rover has reached the safety zone edge and is drilling. Ground analysis indicates it is core-sampling the ice-rich regolith stratum identified in your Chandrayaan-1 data — data publicly shared in 2009. The rover has extracted approximately 3kg of water-ice, constituting 'resource extraction' under the Outer Space Treaty.
INDIA.notes: Water-ice on the Moon is strategic: electrolysis produces hydrogen and oxygen, which are rocket propellants. A nation that controls South Pole ice deposits effectively controls a 'gas station' for cislunar operations. NASA estimates economically recoverable water-ice could sustain a lunar economy for decades.

options.label: File Emergency Action with ICJ + Demand Restitution
options.log: Legal proceedings begin. OPFOR ignores. Sample already extracted.

options.label: Disable OPFOR Rover via Targeted EMP from Orbiter
options.log: OPFOR rover disabled. International condemnation follows. Resource protected.

options.label: Accelerate Indian Extraction — Claim Same Deposit
options.log: India extracts 12kg of ice from same deposit. Legal standoff now bilateral.

INDIA.title: Track 3 — Rover Face-Off at 12 Meters
INDIA.text: Your Chandrayaan rover is physically interposed between the OPFOR machine. OPFOR machine is 340kg — three times your rover mass. It begins a slow push using its excavation arm as a ram. Your rover wheels are spinning in the regolith. 90 seconds before displacement.
INDIA.notes: Physical rover conflict on the lunar surface is unprecedented but the physics are straightforward. In 1/6th Earth gravity, mass still determines push force. Permanently shadowed regions have cryogenically compacted ice-regolith mix that provides better traction than loose upper regolith.

options.label: Sacrifice Rover — Ram OPFOR Drill Mechanism
options.log: Chandrayaan rover destroyed. OPFOR drill arm damaged. Extraction halted. India loses rover.

options.label: Retreat + Broadcast Live Video of OPFOR Aggression
options.log: Worldwide condemnation of OPFOR footage. Diplomatic tide turns.

INDIA.title: Track 3 — Information Victory: 4K Evidence
INDIA.text: The orbital imagery is stunning — 0.5m resolution showing the OPFOR rover national flag, vehicle markings, and drilling in the Indian-designated zone. The footage has gone globally viral within 3 hours. Three UNSC members have called for an emergency session. However, OPFOR ally has announced veto readiness.
INDIA.notes: Remote sensing data from space has been used as legal evidence since the 1986 Rainbow Warrior case. Commercial satellite operators (Maxar, Planet Labs) already provide evidence-as-a-service for conflict zones. India releasing military-grade imagery of a cislunar incident would set a precedent for transparent space governance.

options.label: Share Imagery with All UN Members — Bypass Veto
options.log: General Assembly resolution passed 141-7. Powerful precedent set.

options.label: Negotiate Bilateral Resource-Sharing Agreement
options.log: India and OPFOR negotiate 50/50 extraction rights. De facto partnership begins.

INDIA.title: Track 4 — Lunar Far Side: The Ghost Signal
INDIA.text: Your Chandrayaan-4 relay satellite at Earth-Moon L2 begins receiving burst transmissions at 437.525 MHz. The bursts are 0.3 seconds long, repeat every 47 minutes, and triangulate to a sub-surface origin near Von Karman crater — where China's Chang'e-4 landed in 2019 and is supposedly uninhabited.
INDIA.notes: The Lunar Far Side is permanently shielded from Earth-based radio monitoring — making it the only truly radio-quiet location within the Earth-Moon system. A clandestine communication relay here would be invisible to all Earth-based monitoring, discoverable only by a relay satellite at L2. The Von Karman crater is 186km in diameter and 13km deep — ample space for a sub-surface facility.

options.label: Triangulate Source — Covert Mapping Pass
options.log: Source mapped to 4.7m resolution. Sub-surface cavern confirmed.

options.label: Decode Transmissions — Deploy AI Signal Intelligence
options.log: Burst decoded: military traffic structured as command/control packets.

options.label: Jam the Frequency — Deny Sub-Surface Comms
options.log: Sub-surface base communications jammed. Response unknown.

options.label: Report to COSPAR and ITU — Formal Investigation Request
options.log: International investigation requested. OPFOR stonewalls inspectors.

INDIA.title: Track 4 — Far Side: The Lava Tube Network
INDIA.text: High-resolution GRAIL gravity data reveals the facility is in a network of lava tubes extending 340km, opening near the lunar limb. The facility has been constructed over 4 years using autonomous robotic mining. It appears to contain: a nuclear reactor, a phased array antenna farm, and launch pads for sub-orbital vehicles.
INDIA.notes: Lunar lava tubes have roofs sometimes 100m thick — providing natural radiation shielding equivalent to 10 meters of water, solving the main human survival challenge on the Moon. JAXA's SELENE orbiter confirmed a 50m-wide, 50km-long lava tube entrance near the Marius Hills in 2017. A nuclear reactor on the Moon is not prohibited by the 1967 OST — only nuclear weapons are banned.

options.label: Dispatch Autonomous Scout Probe to Lava Tube Entrance
options.log: Probe deployed. Facility entrance confirmed at lunar coordinates 6.2N, 138.4E.

options.label: Relay Full Intelligence to UNOOSA + Five Eyes
options.log: International community shocked. OPFOR demands India cease provocation.

options.label: Targeted Laser Strike — Disable Reactor Cooling Array
options.log: Reactor cooling array damaged. Facility power output drops 60%.

INDIA.title: Track 4 — Signals Intelligence: Kyber-1024
INDIA.text: Your AI signals intelligence suite determines: the burst transmissions use Kyber-1024, a quantum-resistant encryption algorithm not publicly released until 2023. This means the facility was updated within the last 18 months. The traffic includes time-stamped coordinate packages — consistent with targeting data for cislunar intercept vehicles.
INDIA.notes: Kyber-1024 is a lattice-based cryptographic algorithm selected by NIST in 2022 as a post-quantum encryption standard. Its presence indicates a nation-state with advanced cryptographic capabilities and recent software access. The coordinate data structure matches the format used by cruise missile guidance packages. Running a weapons coordination loop from the lunar far side would make it virtually immune to electronic warfare from Earth.

options.label: Insert Cryptographic Trojan into OPFOR Uplink Stream
options.log: Trojan accepted. India now receives copies of all targeting data.

options.label: Alert UN Secretary-General via Emergency Channel
options.log: UNSC convened. P5 member blocks resolution.

INDIA.title: Track 4 — Jamming Response: Backup Laser Link
INDIA.text: Your 437 MHz jamming silenced the burst transmissions. But 11 hours later, a laser communication terminal on the crater rim points at a previously untracked satellite in a retrograde orbit, re-transmitting via Ka-band to an undisclosed Earth station. You have jammed yourself into revealing their backup communication architecture.
INDIA.notes: Frequency-agile adversaries plan for jamming from the start. A layered communication architecture (RF primary, laser secondary) is considered best practice for any hardened military installation. The retrograde orbit relay is particularly clever — retrograde orbits are rare enough that most SSA sensors are tuned to prograde traffic. The laser link is essentially unjammable without physical access to the terminal line-of-sight.

options.label: Deploy Relay Satellite into Retrograde Orbit — Shadow OPFOR Relay
options.log: Shadow relay deployed. OPFOR laser link intercepted.

options.label: Track Laser Beam — Reverse-Engineer Earth Station Location
options.log: Earth station identified: remote facility in uninhabited desert region.

INDIA.title: Track 5 — NavIC Under Attack: Regional GPS Warfare
INDIA.text: DGCA reports catastrophic NavIC/GPS errors over a 1,200km corridor spanning Ladakh, Nepal, and the Bay of Bengal. Errors range from 200m to 12km. Three Indian Airlines flights have declared navigation emergencies. Satellite imagery shows unusual dust patterns near the Line of Actual Control — consistent with large vehicle columns moving under cover of navigation denial.
INDIA.notes: GPS/NavIC spoofing in conflict zones has been documented in 12 active conflict theaters since 2016, including Syria, Libya, Ukraine, and the South China Sea. The key difference from jamming: spoofing provides false-but-believable coordinates. Aviation autopilots trust GNSS implicitly. India's NavIC system uses L5 military encrypted signals — but civilian aviation uses the unencrypted L1 signal.

options.label: Switch Military Assets to Rubidium Atomic Clock Sync
options.log: Military navigation restored on encrypted signal.

options.label: Issue NOTAM — Ground All Affected Civil Flights
options.log: Civil aviation grounded. Economic cost: Rs 2,400cr/day. Tactically correct.

options.label: Triangulate Spoofing Sources + Deploy EW Strike
options.log: Spoofing arrays on border ridgelines neutralized. 4 towers destroyed.

options.label: Activate Backup INS for Military Units
options.log: Inertial Navigation System activated. Ground forces unaffected by spoofing.

INDIA.title: Track 5 — The Dark Satellite: Retrograde Interceptor
INDIA.text: Byalalu DSN station detects a new object not in yesterday's catalogue. Retrograde orbit at 650km altitude, approaching the IRNSS/NavIC constellation at relative velocity 14.8 km/s. The object is 80cm x 40cm x 60cm, consistent with an ASAT interceptor body.
INDIA.notes: Retrograde orbital insertion costs roughly twice the fuel of a prograde launch. The US, China, and Russia are the only nations confirmed to have conducted retrograde satellite launches. The interceptor configuration matches the size class of China's SC-19 and Russia's Nudol ASAT kill vehicles. At 14.8 km/s relative velocity, even a 1kg mass delivers kinetic energy equivalent to 50kg of TNT.

options.label: Emergency Constellation Maneuver — Scatter IRNSS Fleet
options.log: NavIC constellation dispersed. ASAT passes through empty space.

options.label: Engage with Ground-Based Directed Energy Weapon
options.log: Thermal kill vehicle disabled. Proximity detonation: 400m miss distance.

options.label: Deploy Chaff Cloud — Radar Decoys
options.log: Interceptor confused by chaff. Detonates 2.3km off-target.

options.label: Kinetic ASAT Intercept — Hit the Interceptor
options.log: Interceptor destroyed. Significant debris at 650km LEO created.

INDIA.title: Track 5 — EW Strike Aftermath: Cross-Border Escalation
INDIA.text: Destruction of four spoofing towers on the OPFOR side of the LAC has triggered a diplomatic crisis. OPFOR declared it an 'act of war against sovereign infrastructure.' Their ISR satellites are now conducting intensive imaging of Indian forward positions. The escalation ladder has moved up three rungs.
INDIA.notes: Electronic warfare attacks against ground infrastructure occupy a critical escalation threshold. Unlike jamming (reversible), physically destroying ground infrastructure constitutes a kinetic attack under the laws of armed conflict. India's position depends on whether it can prove the towers were used for military spoofing, versus OPFOR's claim they were civilian telecommunications infrastructure.

options.label: Release Technical Evidence of Spoofing to International Community
options.log: Signal recordings prove towers were military. International opinion shifts.

options.label: Acknowledge Action + Propose Immediate De-escalation
options.log: Back-channel diplomacy begins. Ceasefire on navigation warfare proposed.

options.label: Reinforce Forward Positions While OPFOR is Diplomatically Occupied
options.log: Ground forces advance 12km. Space-enabled ISR guides the operation.

INDIA.title: Track 6 — Solar Storm: X-Class Flare Warning
INDIA.text: NOAA's Space Weather Prediction Center issues an Extreme (X9.8) solar flare warning. Expected CME arrival: 18 hours. At this intensity, unprotected GEO satellites face 40% probability of permanent single-event latchup; LEO satellites in the South Atlantic Anomaly face 70% chance of processor corruption. India's entire fleet of 55 operational satellites is at risk.
INDIA.notes: The 1989 Quebec solar storm caused a 9-hour blackout across Canada by inducing currents in power lines. For satellites, the primary risks are: (1) Single-Event Upset — a cosmic ray flips a bit in memory; (2) Single-Event Latchup — a semiconductor permanently short-circuits; (3) Differential charging causing electrostatic discharge. The most cost-effective mitigation is safe mode — edge-on to the sun, non-critical systems off.

options.label: Orient All Satellites Edge-On to Sun (Safe Mode)
options.log: Fleet survives with 3% permanent hardware loss. Strategic ops paused 18 hours.

options.label: Emergency Power-Down of Non-Critical Systems
options.log: All satellites survive. 24-hour operational pause.

options.label: Maintain Operations — Accept Risk on Military Priority Satellites
options.log: Three satellites permanently damaged. Military ISR maintained through storm.

options.label: Use Storm Window to Covertly Reposition Fleet (OPFOR SSA Blinded)
options.log: Solar storm EMI blinds OPFOR tracking radars. Fleet repositioned undetected.

INDIA.title: Track 7 — Byalalu DSN: Command Ghosting
INDIA.text: The Deep Space Network station at Byalalu detects 'Command Ghosting': unauthorized telecommand packets injected into the Mars Orbiter Mission uplink beam, accepted by MOM's legacy transponder. Ghost commands are directing MOM thrusters to fire in short bursts — slowly exhausting its remaining fuel budget.
INDIA.notes: Command Ghosting requires: (1) precise knowledge of the spacecraft uplink frequency and modulation (available in ISRO technical papers); (2) physical proximity to the uplink beam path; (3) knowledge of the command format. MOM uses an S-band uplink at 7.19 GHz. Its authentication was designed for the 2013 threat environment. Modern software-defined radio costing under $5,000 could replicate MOM command format from ISRO published documents.

options.label: Purge Command Buffer + Re-authenticate Entire Session
options.log: Ghost commands deleted. Enhanced authentication activated.

options.label: Physical Uplink Disconnect — Autonomous Mode
options.log: MOM enters autonomous survival mode. 72-hour comms blackout.

options.label: Feed False Telemetry — Let Ghost Think It Succeeded
options.log: MOM simulates thruster response. Attacker believes they have fuel control. Trap set.

options.label: Trace Injection Beam — Geolocation of OPFOR Antenna
options.log: Antenna located: rooftop installation in neutral-country city.

INDIA.title: Track 7 — DSN: Multi-Vector Ground Assault
INDIA.text: While containing the Byalalu attack, two additional DSN assets come under coordinated attack: Port Blair ISTRAC receives a 4.7 Tbps DDoS attack; simultaneously, a drone swarm of 12 commercial quadrotors modified with RF jammers approaches the Lucknow ground station antenna farm.
INDIA.notes: Ground segment attacks are 1,000 times cheaper than developing ASAT weapons but can achieve similar effects. Port Blair ISTRAC is critical for LEO satellite passes over the eastern Indian Ocean — its loss creates a 6-hour daily coverage gap for Indian naval ISR. The drone swarm uses off-the-shelf quadrotors with aftermarket RF modules — total cost under $50,000.

options.label: Activate Backup Satellite-Direct Relay (Skip Ground Station)
options.log: Satellite-to-satellite relay activated. Ground segment bypassed.

options.label: Deploy Anti-Drone EW Systems at Lucknow Perimeter
options.log: Drone swarm neutralized. 11 of 12 drones forced down.

options.label: Emergency Routing via Commercial SatCom (Intelsat)
options.log: Commercial routing established. Security compromised. Operations continue.

INDIA.title: Track 7 — Ghost Trap: Reverse the Hunt
INDIA.text: The deception is working. MOM fake telemetry convinced the ghost attacker they have fuel authority. They issued a 'deorbit burn' command sequence. India executes a pre-planned 'diagnostic burn' moving MOM to a higher orbit instead. Confused, the attacker increases injection power — making their signal much easier to direction-find.
INDIA.notes: Direction-finding (DF) of a hostile uplink injection requires multiple receiving stations working in synchrony — Time Difference of Arrival (TDOA). ISRO has three stations with DF capability (Byalalu, Lucknow, Port Blair) forming a triangle across the subcontinent. TDOA from three stations can locate an RF source to within 500m accuracy. The ghost attacker's increased power is operationally fatal — they revealed themselves for the sake of an already-doomed operation.

options.label: DF Lock — Share Location with National Security Agencies
options.log: Attacker location confirmed. NSA and RAW notified. Physical arrest operation planned.

options.label: Maintain Deception — Harvest Full Attack Methodology
options.log: Full command injection methodology documented. Vulnerability patched globally.

INDIA.title: Track 7 — Attribution: The Neutral Country Problem
INDIA.text: The command injection antenna is on the 14th floor of a commercial building in Kathmandu — a neutral country with an open border treaty with India. The antenna is registered to a telecom company majority-owned by an OPFOR state enterprise through three shell companies. Confronting Nepal risks a diplomatic crisis; ignoring it allows the attack to continue.
INDIA.notes: Using neutral-country territory for military space operations is a well-established grey zone tactic. During the Cold War, Soviet intelligence used satellite-receiving facilities in neutral Finland to intercept Western communications. A 7.19 GHz high-gain antenna needed to inject commands into ISRO uplink would require a 2.4m dish — easily concealed on a commercial rooftop with the right building permits.

options.label: Share Evidence with Nepal Government — Request Shutdown
options.log: Nepal shuts down installation. OPFOR loses injection capability. Diplomatic friction: moderate.

options.label: Unilateral Electronic Countermeasure — Burn the Uplink
options.log: High-power counter-signal disables OPFOR antenna. Nepal protests frequency interference.

options.label: Covert Physical Operation — Disable Antenna
options.log: Antenna physically disabled by covert team. Plausible deniability maintained.

INDIA.title: Track 8 — Orbital Manufacturing: Firmware Sabotage
INDIA.text: India's micro-gravity manufacturing platform in LEO reports a thermal anomaly in its main furnace chamber — 340C above setpoint in 90 seconds. Remote diagnostics reveal the firmware controlling thermal management has been replaced with a modified version using a stolen ISRO developer certificate to bypass digital signature checks.
INDIA.notes: Micro-gravity manufacturing produces materials impossible to create on Earth due to convective currents. Ultra-pure ZBLAN fluoride fiber optic cables produced in space have 100x lower attenuation than terrestrial fiber — each meter is worth $5,000-50,000. The stolen developer certificate indicates the attack was prepared months or years in advance, with access to ISRO software signing infrastructure.

options.label: Deploy AI Threat Hunter — Autonomous Firmware Rollback
options.log: Malicious firmware identified and rolled back. Manufacturing resumed in 6 hours.

options.label: Emergency Thermal Dump — Vent Furnace to Space
options.log: Furnace vented. 3-week production loss. Platform structurally safe.

options.label: Isolate Platform Network — Operate in Manual Mode
options.log: Platform operates manually. Productivity drops 70% but is hack-proof.

options.label: Revoke All Stolen Certificates + Force Re-authentication Across Fleet
options.log: Certificate revocation complete. OPFOR loses all developer-level access to ISRO networks.

INDIA.title: Track 9 — Z-Point Standoff: 50 Meters
INDIA.text: An OPFOR inspector satellite has shadowed your GSAT-30 relay for 51 hours and is now within 50 meters. At this range, both spacecraft are within each other's thruster plume zones. The OPFOR craft has begun transmitting on GSAT-30 S-band uplink frequency, causing interference with the primary military link to Western Naval Command.
INDIA.notes: At 50-meter separation in GEO orbit, the two spacecraft are in each other's proximity interference zones: (1) the OPFOR craft cold-gas thrusters contaminate your solar panels with propellant residue; (2) its antennas radiate power directly onto your solar cells. The OPFOR craft is maintaining exactly 50m separation via extremely precise station-keeping, demonstrating highly sophisticated orbital mechanics.

options.label: Static Discharge Pulse — EMP via Deployed Tether
options.log: OPFOR electronics temporarily disabled. Inspector drifts to 400m separation.

options.label: Release Xenon Gas Cloud — Obscure Sensors
options.log: OPFOR optical and IR sensors obscured. 4-hour operational window created.

options.label: Rapid Orbit Change — 500m Radial Maneuver
options.log: GSAT-30 moves to new slot. OPFOR requires 3.2 hours to match.

options.label: Broadcast OPFOR Interference Attempts on ITU Channels
options.log: International monitoring confirms OPFOR interference. Formal citation issued.

INDIA.title: Track 10 — Asteroid M-Class: Cislunar Race
INDIA.text: Asteroid 2025 JX7 — an M-type body approximately 85 meters diameter — has been captured into a temporary cislunar orbit. Spectroscopic analysis indicates platinum-group metals at approximately $4.7 trillion market equivalent. An OPFOR mining tug is matching the asteroid velocity and preparing to deploy anchoring harpoons. Your cislunar prospector vehicle is 22 hours away.
INDIA.notes: M-type asteroids are metallic — primarily iron-nickel with platinum-group element concentrations 10-1,000x higher than Earth's richest mines. Under the 2015 US Commercial Space Launch Competitiveness Act and equivalent Indian legislation, extracted resources belong to the extracting entity — but the celestial body itself cannot be owned. This legal framework was designed to encourage asteroid mining while respecting the OST.

options.label: Sprint Burn — Arrive in 14 Hours (Exhaust Reserve Fuel)
options.log: Prospector arrives before OPFOR completes harpoon deployment.

options.label: Deploy Kinetic Harpoon from Long Range
options.log: Harpoon anchors asteroid. India claim physically established.

options.label: Declare Scientific Preserve — Petition UN COPUOS
options.log: Legal claim filed. OPFOR continues docking operations during proceedings.

options.label: Negotiate Joint Extraction Agreement (50/50)
options.log: Framework agreement reached. Historic joint space resource treaty signed.

INDIA.title: Track 10 — Asteroid: The Tug-of-War
INDIA.text: Both your prospector and OPFOR mining tug are now anchored to opposite faces of 2025 JX7. Both are attempting to redirect the asteroid trajectory. The asteroid is a loose rubble pile (porosity approximately 50%) — two competing tugs have destabilized its rotation and it is now tumbling at 2 rotations per hour.
INDIA.notes: Rubble-pile asteroids (the majority of near-Earth asteroids in this size class) have no structural integrity — held together by gravity and weak inter-particle cohesion. Simultaneous anchor by two independent harpoon systems creates competing structural loads. A full uncontrolled tumble could disintegrate the asteroid — destroying all vehicles attached to it.

options.label: Cease Burn — Propose Joint L4 Insertion
options.log: OPFOR agrees. Joint operation inserts asteroid at L4. Historic cooperation.

options.label: Increase Thrust — Win the Tug-of-War
options.log: India wins. Asteroid on L4 trajectory. OPFOR tug detaches at fuel limit.

options.label: Detach Own Tug — Alert Planetary Defense Authority
options.log: International alarm raised. OPFOR ordered to cease. Asteroid trajectory stabilized.

INDIA.title: Track 11 — Orbital Hotel: Civilian Hostage Crisis
INDIA.text: A commercial orbital station hosting 23 paying guests including 4 Indian nationals experiences catastrophic attitude control failure after a suspected cyberattack on its propulsion computer. The station is tumbling at 0.7 revolutions per minute. Rescue timeline: 14 hours minimum. OPFOR has offered a 'rescue mission' that would give them access to the station's data systems.
INDIA.notes: Orbital stations are covered by the 1968 Rescue Agreement, which mandates all spacefaring nations assist in rescue operations. The tumble rate of 0.7 rpm will cause nausea and disorientation in about 40 minutes. The presence of OPFOR nationals on the station creates a legal obligation for the attacking party to rescue its own citizens — a politically awkward situation if OPFOR is the attacker.

options.label: Authorize OPFOR Rescue — Accept Verification Conditions
options.log: OPFOR Soyuz docks. Hostages rescued. Intel compromise: unknown.

options.label: Sprint Indian Crew Vehicle (Gaganyaan) — 11-Hour Window
options.log: Gaganyaan crew arrives in 11 hours. All 23 rescued. India is hero of the hour.

options.label: Remotely Stabilize Station Via Attitude Control Hack
options.log: India space cyber team remotely stabilizes tumble. Crisis averted without physical rescue.

INDIA.title: Track 11 — Cislunar Highway: The Traffic Problem
INDIA.text: 47 vehicles are currently in transit between Earth-Moon L1 and L2 Lagrange points, coordinated by no single authority. India's Chandrayaan supply shuttle detects a head-on approach by an OPFOR cargo tug with a 340-meter miss distance — within Conjunction Warning thresholds. The OPFOR tug does not respond to ITU emergency frequencies and cannot maneuver without aborting its mission.
INDIA.notes: Cislunar space has no equivalent to international air traffic control. Unlike LEO, there are no designated lanes and no collision avoidance authority. A collision at cislunar distances would create debris in a very high-energy orbit — potentially raining fragments on both the Moon and Earth over years.

options.label: Execute Avoidance Burn — Chandrayaan Shuttle Maneuvers
options.log: Collision avoided. India absorbs fuel cost. Mission delayed 6 hours.

options.label: Broadcast Universal Distress + Force OPFOR Tug Hohmann Abort
options.log: OPFOR tug aborts transfer. Both vehicles safe. OPFOR mission fails. Diplomatic incident.

options.label: Propose Emergency Cislunar Traffic Management Protocol
options.log: India tables cislunar traffic treaty. Becomes framework for Artemis Traffic Authority.

INDIA.title: Track 12 — Rogue Reactor: Cosmos-1818 Coolant Leak
INDIA.text: A 1980s-era Soviet RTG satellite — Cosmos-1818 — has developed an uncontrolled tumble and is leaking liquid sodium-potassium coolant into an 850km orbit corridor heavily used by India's RISAT constellation. The coolant droplets range from 0.5mm to 5cm — invisible to ground-based radar but capable of penetrating satellite shielding at orbital velocities.
INDIA.notes: Cosmos-1818 is a real Soviet naval radar reconnaissance satellite launched in 1987, cooled by liquid sodium-potassium (NaK) alloy. ESA documented over 10,000 such droplets in a 1998 study. At orbital velocity (~7.5 km/s), a 1cm NaK droplet carries kinetic energy equivalent to a hand grenade. This is not a hypothetical threat — it is actively affecting satellite operations today.

options.label: Raise RISAT Constellation Orbit Above 880km
options.log: Constellation repositioned. Fuel budget for mission extension reduced by 30%.

options.label: Petition ROSCOSMOS to Deorbit Cosmos-1818
options.log: Russia agrees in principle. Engineering assessment: 18 months to deorbit.

options.label: Deploy Active Debris Removal Vehicle (Harpoon + Deorbit Kit)
options.log: ADR vehicle grapples Cosmos-1818 and initiates controlled reentry. Success.

INDIA.title: Track 12 — OPFOR Nuclear Power Satellite
INDIA.text: Intelligence confirms OPFOR has launched a Nuclear Power Source satellite into a 1,100km orbit directly above India's densest LEO constellation band. The satellite uses a fission reactor producing 150kW — consistent with powering a high-power EW payload or directed-energy weapon. The 1992 UN Principles on NPS prohibit this orbit height.
INDIA.notes: The 1992 UN Principles Relevant to the Use of Nuclear Power Sources require NPS satellites to operate in sufficiently high orbits — typically above 800km for short-duration missions. However, the principles are non-binding recommendations, not treaty obligations. A 150kW nuclear-powered EW satellite would represent a 10x increase in power over current chemical battery-based EW systems.

options.label: File Emergency UN Protest — Cite 1992 NPS Principles
options.log: UN acknowledges but cannot enforce. OPFOR satellite remains.

options.label: Develop Shielded NPS-Hardened Satellite Variants
options.log: Next-generation satellite design prioritizes radiation hardening. 3-year program initiated.

options.label: Deploy Co-Orbital Inspector to Characterize Payload
options.log: Inspector confirms: directed-energy weapon. Technical specifications transmitted to allies.

INDIA.title: Track 13 — Quantum Communication: The Entanglement Race
INDIA.text: India's quantum communication satellite (QCS-1) is ready for deployment — using quantum key distribution via entangled photon pairs. Before activation, ISRO receives an anonymous tip: a listening post near Leh has detected an identical quantum channel signature already active in the orbit — someone is operating a QKD satellite intercepting QCS-1 planned beam path.
INDIA.notes: Quantum Key Distribution works by sending individual photons as key bits. Any interception collapses the photon state, revealing the eavesdropper. However, a sophisticated attack called 'Detector Blinding' floods the receiving detector with bright light, causing it to operate classically rather than quantum-mechanically, allowing interception without detection. China demonstrated the first satellite-to-ground QKD link in 2017 via the Micius satellite.

options.label: Deploy QCS-1 on Modified Frequency — Avoid Intercept Beam
options.log: QCS-1 online on alternate photon wavelength. OPFOR intercept defeated.

options.label: Delay QCS-1 — Investigate Unknown QKD Satellite First
options.log: Unknown satellite characterized. Commercial QKD experiment — not hostile.

options.label: Activate QCS-1 + Exploit Intercept for Reverse Engineering
options.log: India reverse-engineers OPFOR quantum intercept methodology. Vulnerability patched globally.

INDIA.title: Track 13 — Quantum Race: The Enigma Dilemma
INDIA.text: India quantum-secured channel is established. Intercepts confirm: OPFOR entire Tier-1 military satellite communication network uses AES-256 that India's quantum computers can theoretically break in 9 hours. The question: use this advantage covertly, or disclose it to force OPFOR to upgrade — eliminating the advantage.
INDIA.notes: The NIST quantum computing threat timeline (2024) estimates that fault-tolerant quantum computers capable of breaking RSA-2048 in hours will exist by 2030-2035. AES-256 is theoretically vulnerable to Grover's algorithm on a sufficiently large quantum computer. This is identical to the Enigma dilemma: Churchill sometimes allowed Allied ships to be sunk rather than reveal Britain had broken Enigma.

options.label: Use Covertly — Exploit Intelligence Advantage for Maximum Gain
options.log: India has advance warning of every OPFOR space operation. Strategic victory assured.

options.label: Disclose Vulnerability to OPFOR via Neutral Back-Channel
options.log: Goodwill gesture accepted. Diplomatic channel opens. OPFOR upgrades encryption.

options.label: Share with Allies Only — Form Quantum Intelligence Coalition
options.log: Five-nation quantum intelligence alliance formed. OPFOR space operations now fully transparent.

INDIA.title: Track 14 — Space-Based Solar Power: Strategic Energy Weapon
INDIA.text: India's first Space-Based Solar Power demonstration satellite (SolarBharat-1) is operational at GEO, transmitting 100MW via microwave beam to a rectenna farm in Rajasthan. OPFOR has identified the microwave beam as a potential directed-energy weapon and is demanding India shut down the program.
INDIA.notes: Space-Based Solar Power has been studied since Peter Glaser's 1968 proposal. A 2GW SBSP system would use a microwave beam approximately 10km in diameter at Earth's surface, with intensity levels far below biological safety limits. OPFOR's demand sets a dangerous precedent: by the same logic, any rocket program could be shut down because rockets can carry warheads.

options.label: Reject OPFOR Demand — Cite Civilian Power Priority
options.log: India refuses to shut down. OPFOR escalates to UN.

options.label: Accept International Inspection Regime for SolarBharat-1
options.log: Transparency confidence-building measure accepted. OPFOR concerns partially satisfied.

options.label: Demonstrate Weaponization is Impossible — Technical Briefing to UNOSA
options.log: Technical evidence presented. UNOSA accepts civilian nature of program.

options.label: Threaten to Aim at OPFOR Capital — Coercive Deterrence
options.log: Massive escalation. International community condemns India. UNSC emergency session.

INDIA.title: Track 15 — Final Sovereignty: ASAT Saturation Launch
INDIA.text: Intelligence from multiple sources confirms OPFOR has loaded and fueled six ASAT missiles at two launch sites, targeting India's highest-priority military satellites. Launch window opens in 90 minutes. OPFOR ambassador to the UN has just walked out of an emergency session. The National Security Council is in emergency session.
INDIA.notes: A saturation ASAT launch is the space equivalent of a nuclear first strike — it permanently degrades the victim's space capabilities. The 2019 Indian Mission Shakti ASAT test showed India has the technical capability to respond in kind. However, an Indian ASAT response would create debris clouds that India's own constellation must pass through repeatedly. The mathematics of mutual assured space destruction closely mirror nuclear MAD.

options.label: Global Ceasefire Proposal via Emergency UN Channel
options.log: Diplomatic victory. Space assets preserved for all humanity. India as space peace leader.

options.label: Pre-emptive Defensive Strike — Ground OPFOR ASAT Launchers
options.log: Conventional strike neutralizes launchers. Escalation risk: extreme.

options.label: Full Retaliatory ASAT Strike + Debris Cloud
options.log: Mutual space destruction. LEO unusable for 40 years. Both programs crippled.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 1 — PRSS-1 Dazzling: Opening Gambit
PAKISTAN.text: SUPARCO SSA cell detects that PRSS-1 is receiving periodic laser illumination from an unknown ground-based source in the Kashmir valley. The laser is bleaching the CCD detector array during the 4-minute targeting window — rendering imagery unusable over sensitive regions near Siachen.
PAKISTAN.notes: Ground-based laser dazzling is one of the most cost-effective ASAT techniques: a 50W green laser costing $15,000 can render a $500M satellite camera unusable during its overpass. China has been suspected of dazzling a US satellite from its Xinjiang facility in 2006. The dazzling is timed to the satellite overpass — meaning the attacker has precise ephemeris data for PRSS-1, likely from its published NORAD two-line element set.

options.label: Deploy Automatic Optical Shutter (Occulting Mask)
options.log: Shutter closes during laser illumination. Imagery quality maintained for 80% of overpass.

options.label: Counter-Dazzle — Reflect Laser Back to Source
options.log: Retroreflector array deployed. Return beam disables source laser optics.

options.label: Repoint Satellite Pitch Axis +15 Degrees — Change Vulnerability Window
options.log: Modified overpass geometry reduces dazzle exposure to 40 seconds.

options.label: Geolocate Laser Source + Forward to Army Air Defence
options.log: Source triangulated to grid reference. Artillery mission authorized.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 1 — Dazzle Countermeasures: OPFOR Upgrades
PAKISTAN.text: India has detected your counter-measures and is now using an adaptive laser system that changes wavelength between passes — cycling through green (532nm), red (660nm), and near-infrared (808nm). Your fixed-wavelength shutter is only calibrated for green. The new multi-spectral attack is degrading PRSS-1's multispectral imager across all bands. However, your radar imaging mode (SAR) remains operational.
PAKISTAN.notes: Multi-spectral laser dazzling requires either multi-wavelength notch filters (very expensive to retrofit) or a broadband shutter that also prevents any imaging. The SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) fallback is significant: SAR operates at C-band (5.6cm wavelength), completely immune to optical dazzling, and can see through clouds and at night. PRSS-1's SAR resolution is approximately 3 meters — adequate for tracking large military formations.

options.label: Switch to SAR Mode — Accept Resolution Limitation
options.log: SAR imaging provides 3m resolution. Large convoy movements tracked effectively.

options.label: Install Broadband Notch Filter via Remote Software Command
options.log: Firmware update installs adaptive filter. Full spectrum coverage restored at 70% efficiency.

options.label: Request Emergency Imaging Tasking from Chinese Commercial Satellites
options.log: Jilin-1 constellation provides 0.75m optical imagery as backup. Chinese intelligence shared.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 1 — Kinetic Escalation: The Artillery Strike
PAKISTAN.text: The Army Air Defence has fired on the laser facility. Post-strike BDA from PRSS-1 SAR confirms: the target was a hillside observatory — but three laser systems were present, not one. Two have survived the strike and are now operational. Additionally, the Indian Army has moved a mobile ASAT unit (Prithvi Air Defence) to the Kashmir valley.
PAKISTAN.notes: The Prithvi Air Defence (PAD) system uses a modified Prithvi-II missile as a kinetic ASAT interceptor. It achieved a successful exo-atmospheric intercept in 2006 during Missile Defence testing. The presence of PAD in Kashmir signals India's intent to use kinetic ASAT capability — escalating the conflict from dazzling (a nuisance) to existential threat to Pakistan's entire space program. The mistaken identity of the laser facility creates a legal and moral complication for Pakistan's narrative.

options.label: Acknowledge Strike Error — Propose Ceasefire
options.log: Diplomatic admission. De-escalation begins. Strategic credibility damaged.

options.label: Activate Space Domain Deterrence — Alert ASAT Posture
options.log: Pakistan raises ASAT readiness. India pauses PAD deployment. Tense standoff.

options.label: Emergency ISR Surge — Find Remaining Two Laser Sites
options.log: Both remaining sites identified. Strike option held in reserve as leverage.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 2 — GSO Slot Crisis: Dead Satellite Drift
PAKISTAN.text: SUPARCO receives urgent notification from the ITU: a defunct Indian GSAT-12 (decommissioned 2019) has begun an uncontrolled drift from its old slot toward Pakistan's assigned 38E slot, currently occupied by PAKSAT-1R. Collision probability in 72 hours accumulates to concerning levels. India says it has no fuel to maneuver the dead satellite.
PAKISTAN.notes: Dead GEO satellites ('zombie satellites') are a growing crisis. The ITU's Radio Regulations require operators to maneuver GEO satellites to a 'graveyard orbit' (300km above GEO) at end-of-life — but compliance is voluntary and poorly enforced. If GSAT-12 drifts into Pakistan's 38E slot, their slight velocity differences create collision risk over time.

options.label: Close Proximity Scan of GSAT-12 — Assess Residual Fuel
options.log: GSAT-12 has 2.3kg of residual hydrazine. Enough for a 0.3 m/s push.

options.label: Push GSAT-12 to Graveyard Orbit (Charitable Disposal)
options.log: Pakistan performs unprecedented third-party satellite disposal. ITU commends.

options.label: File Emergency Coordination Claim — Demand India Fund Disposal
options.log: Legal proceeding begins. Takes 2 years. Satellite drifts further meanwhile.

options.label: Extract Intelligence from GSAT-12 During Proximity Scan
options.log: GSAT-12 transponder memory holds encryption keys from its operational life. Intelligence windfall.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 2 — GEO Housekeeping: Accidental Contact
PAKISTAN.text: During the proximity operation on GSAT-12, PAKSAT-1R inspector arm accidentally makes contact with GSAT-12 antenna mount. Unexpectedly, GSAT-12 attitude control system — running on emergency backup battery — briefly activates and fires a thruster burst. For 8 minutes, PAKSAT-1R was physically touching GSAT-12. This is the first known physical contact between two nations' satellites without prior agreement. India has noticed.
PAKISTAN.notes: Physical contact between satellites triggers the 1972 Liability Convention contact clause — the state launching a satellite bears international liability for damage caused by that satellite to another state's space object. Even if PAKSAT-1R caused no damage to GSAT-12, India could argue the contact was intentional. The evidentiary challenge: ground-based radar cannot confirm who initiated contact at GEO distances.

options.label: Report Contact Transparently to ITU — Pre-empt Indian Accusation
options.log: Transparency pays off. India's complaint dismissed for lack of damage evidence.

options.label: Deny Contact Occurred — Claim Indian Telemetry Error
options.log: Deniability attempted. US Space Fence radar independently confirms contact. Pakistan's position collapses.

options.label: Leverage Contact — Claim GSAT-12 Data Recovery Rights
options.log: Novel legal claim filed. Disputed but creates precedent for orbital salvage law.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 2 — GSAT-12 Intelligence Exploit
PAKISTAN.text: The data recovered from GSAT-12 transponder memory is extraordinary: steganographic data storage hidden within routine telemetry packets contains archived intelligence reports from ISRO's classified payload. The encryption uses a key derived from GSAT-12's own serial number — a known weakness in legacy satellite cryptography. Within 4 hours, Pakistan's signals intelligence team has decrypted 847GB of data covering 6 years of Indian space intelligence operations.
PAKISTAN.notes: Steganographic data storage in satellite telemetry is a documented technique used by intelligence agencies to covertly transmit data hidden within normal engineering data streams. Hardware-derived keys (serial numbers, manufacturing codes) are a well-documented legacy cryptography weakness — the same vulnerability affected early RFID payment cards and US military SINCGARS radios. The 847GB haul would represent one of the largest peacetime intelligence captures in South Asian history.

options.label: Exploit Intelligence Immediately — Operational Advantage
options.log: Pakistan gains 3-year advance awareness of Indian space program plans. Strategic windfall.

options.label: Sell Intelligence to Allies (China, Turkey) — Revenue + Diplomatic Capital
options.log: Intelligence sold. $200M in defense credit received. India-China intelligence gap widened.

options.label: Preserve as Deterrence — Notify India Covertly That You Have It
options.log: Pakistan signals possession of data without disclosure. India's space program enters emergency review.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 3 — Lunar Landing: The Un-notified Neighbor
PAKISTAN.text: Pakistan's first lunar lander (PakLuna-1), launched as part of the China-Pakistan space cooperation framework, touches down in the Lunar South Pole's Nobile crater — 7km from the Indian Chandrayaan-4 landing site. During final descent, PakLuna-1's radar altimeter detected what appears to be a previously undisclosed structure beneath the surface — buried approximately 2.4 meters down, with a metallic radar return signature consistent with a habitat module.
PAKISTAN.notes: The Outer Space Treaty (1967) Article IX requires states to avoid 'harmful contamination' and mandates consultation if activities might interfere with others' operations. The 'un-notified structure' creates a legal puzzle: if India constructed it without COSPAR disclosure, India is in violation of Treaty transparency requirements. The 2.4m burial depth is consistent with construction using a robotic boring machine — achievable with current technology in 8-12 months of continuous operation.

options.label: Publicize Radar Discovery — File OST Transparency Violation
options.log: UN notified. India denies knowledge. COSPAR inspection requested.

options.label: Send PakLuna Rover to Excavate + Document the Structure
options.log: Excavation reveals a Chinese autonomous construction module from a secret 2023 mission.

options.label: Jam Structure's Presumed Relay Comms — Isolate It
options.log: Sub-surface structure loses communications. Operators on Earth begin emergency protocols.

options.label: Coordinate with China — Share Discovery Secretly
options.log: China provides classified briefing: the structure belongs to a third party, not China or India.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 3 — Lunar Sovereignty: The Flagless Miner
PAKISTAN.text: COSPAR emergency inspection reveals the sub-surface structure is an automated mining station of unknown origin. Its electronic components use commercial off-the-shelf parts available from multiple nations — deliberate obfuscation. It has been extracting titanium ore from lunar regolith for approximately 14 months. Someone has been running a covert lunar mining operation.
PAKISTAN.notes: Covert automated lunar resource extraction represents a new category of space law violation. The 1967 OST prohibits national appropriation of celestial bodies but says nothing about fully automated, flagless resource operations. Attribution requires forensic analysis of electronic components, propulsion chemistry, and orbital mechanics — all of which can be deliberately obfuscated. This scenario mirrors the 'flagless privateer' problem of maritime law in the 17th century.

options.label: Claim Ownership via Finder's Rights — Occupy the Facility
options.log: PakLuna occupies facility. First 'captured' lunar infrastructure. Unprecedented legal claim.

options.label: Disable the Station — Preserve the Site as Evidence
options.log: Station powered down. All evidence preserved for international inspection.

options.label: Establish Physical Presence — Control Access to the Site
options.log: PakLuna secures perimeter. Pakistan controls access to the most significant lunar discovery since 1969.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 3 — The Chinese Confession
PAKISTAN.text: China has provided a classified briefing: the sub-surface module is a Chinese National Space Administration autonomous construction unit, deployed secretly in 2023 as part of a classified program to pre-position infrastructure for a 2035 crewed lunar base. China asks Pakistan to maintain secrecy and offers: full data-sharing from the mining operation, preferred partnership in the 2035 base, and advanced hypersonic defense technology.
PAKISTAN.notes: If Pakistan conceals a Chinese OST violation, it becomes complicit in that violation. However, the offered technology (hypersonic defense) would give Pakistan a generational military advantage over India. The lunar base partnership would give Pakistan access to cislunar infrastructure it could not afford independently. The strategic calculus is brutal: complicity vs. alliance value.

options.label: Accept Chinese Terms — Maintain Secrecy
options.log: Pakistan receives hypersonic tech and lunar base partnership. OST violation concealed.

options.label: Decline — Disclose Violation to UNOOSA
options.log: Pakistan discloses Chinese violation. China-Pakistan relations severely strained. Pakistan gains international credibility.

options.label: Negotiate Better Terms — Demand Additional Technology Package
options.log: China increases offer: quantum communication technology + 30% of mined titanium revenue.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 3 — Silent Station: The Ghost Goes Active
PAKISTAN.text: Jamming the structure's relay has had an unexpected effect: a dormant safety protocol has activated, and the station has begun a 'self-preservation burn' — its robotic excavation arm has emerged from the surface and is deploying a small antenna array directly toward PakLuna-1. PakLuna's telemetry shows unusual power drain, as if something is inductively charging from your rover's power bus.
PAKISTAN.notes: Inductive power transfer between space objects in proximity is theoretically possible at close range using resonant magnetic coupling, similar to wireless phone charging but scaled for space. The self-preservation behavior suggests advanced autonomous decision-making — the station is acting to preserve itself, raising profound questions about the legal status of autonomous systems that act without human direction in space.

options.label: Emergency PakLuna Retreat — Escape Inductive Range
options.log: PakLuna retreats 200m. Power drain ceases. Station continues autonomous operation.

options.label: Hard Power Cutoff — Battery Isolation — Force Station to Drain Itself
options.log: PakLuna enters dead-battery mode. Station searches for power elsewhere — arm extended but finds nothing.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 4 — Electronic Warfare: NavIC Spoofing Operation
PAKISTAN.text: SIGINT Command presents an operational option: spoofing India's regional NavIC signals over a 300km corridor along the Line of Control to mask a Pakistani Army logistics movement of 140 vehicles toward forward positions. The spoofing would require 4 hours of sustained emission from three mountaintop transmitters in Azad Kashmir. Success probability: 78%. Collateral effect: civil aviation errors in a 600km radius.
PAKISTAN.notes: GNSS spoofing at military scale requires sustained, high-power, directional transmissions — fundamentally different from cheap civilian spoofing seen in the Middle East. Military-grade spoofing systems used by Iran (which captured a US RQ-170 Sentinel drone in 2011) generate signals indistinguishable from legitimate GNSS broadcasts to standard receivers. The collateral effect on civil aviation is the primary ethical and legal constraint — ICAO defines spoofing of navigation signals as a potential war crime if it causes civilian casualties.

options.label: Execute Full Spoofing Operation
options.log: Logistics movement masked. Indian ISR sees ghost convoy 40km west of actual position.

options.label: Execute Limited Spoofing — Military Frequencies Only
options.log: Military-band spoofing only. Civil aviation unaffected. Reduced tactical effect.

options.label: Maintain Neutrality — Use Terrain Masking Instead
options.log: Logistics movement uses terrain masking and night movement instead of EW.

options.label: Spoof + Simultaneously Jam Indian SAR Satellites
options.log: Combined EW operation. India's ISR completely blind. Movement undetected.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 4 — NavIC Aftermath: Quantum Clock Detection
PAKISTAN.text: India's NavIC monitoring network has detected the spoofing emission within 2.5 hours — faster than expected, because India deployed a new quantum-clock verification system that detects timing inconsistencies in spoofed signals. Indian SIGINT has direction-found the three transmitters and has shared coordinates with Indian Air Force. IAF strike aircraft are 20 minutes from the transmitter sites. Pakistan's Army Air Defence has locked radar on the approaching aircraft.
PAKISTAN.notes: Quantum clock verification for GNSS authentication works by comparing the quantum phase of received signals against an ultra-precise atomic clock reference. A spoofed signal — no matter how well crafted — will have timing errors at the nanosecond level that reveal its artificial origin. The technology was publicly described in 2022 papers from NIST. India's rapid deployment of this technology creates an asymmetric advantage.

options.label: Ceasefire — Immediately Shut Down Transmitters
options.log: Transmitters offline. IAF recalled. Escalation averted. Strategic advantage surrendered.

options.label: Activate SHORAD — Protect Transmitter Sites
options.log: Air engagement begins. Two IAF aircraft damaged. Conflict escalates to hot war.

options.label: Sacrifice Transmitters — Withdraw and Deny
options.log: Sites abandoned before IAF strike. No Pakistani casualties. EW infrastructure lost.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 4 — Double EW Operation: Third-Party Detection
PAKISTAN.text: The combined NavIC spoofing and SAR jamming operation was remarkably successful for 3 hours. But the jamming of India's RISAT SAR satellite required Pakistan to emit at 50kW power levels from Murree Hills, which was detected by a US signals intelligence satellite on routine overpass. The US has privately warned Pakistan that continued jamming may be shared with India under the Information Sharing Agreement.
PAKISTAN.notes: Electronic warfare against satellites generates radio frequency emissions observable by third-party signals intelligence platforms. The US maintains a global SIGINT network that monitors all major emitters, including adversary EW systems. The US-India Information Sharing Agreement (ISA, signed 2019) includes space situational awareness data. This is the 'great power alignment' problem — non-superpower states conducting space domain operations always risk exposure by third parties with global sensor coverage.

options.label: Immediately Cease Jamming — Comply with US Warning
options.log: Jamming ceases. US-Pakistan relationship preserved. Tactical advantage ended.

options.label: Continue Operations — Call US Bluff
options.log: US shares coordinates with India 6 hours later. Precision airstrike destroys jamming site.

options.label: Shift to Low-Power Directional Jamming — Reduce Observability
options.log: Power reduced 80%. US SIGINT loses track. Jamming effectiveness reduced but continues.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 5 — DSN Severed: Celestial Navigation Emergency
PAKISTAN.text: India has successfully executed a cyberattack on the SUPARCO-NARL ground station at Karachi — the primary telemetry link for PRSS-1 and the backup command facility for PAKSAT-1R. Communications with both satellites have been severed. PAKSAT-1R requires regular station-keeping commands to maintain its GEO slot — without uplink, it will begin drifting within 14 hours.
PAKISTAN.notes: TT&C station redundancy is a fundamental resilience requirement — major space powers have 3-6 global ground stations for each critical satellite. Pakistan's TT&C network has only two stations (Karachi and Lahore), with no international partnering agreement for backup access. This single point of failure was identified in the 2018 SUPARCO infrastructure review but was not remediated due to budget constraints.

options.label: Activate Star Tracker Autonomous Mode on PAKSAT-1R
options.log: Autonomous navigation maintains orbital position to within plus or minus 0.05 degrees.

options.label: Emergency Contact via Chinese Yuanwang Tracking Ship
options.log: Chinese tracking vessel provides backup uplink. PAKSAT-1R commanded remotely via Chinese relay.

options.label: Manual Burn — Accept Position Error
options.log: Blind burn executed. PAKSAT-1R drifts 0.3 degrees from slot. ITU coordination required.

options.label: Counter-Hack — Restore Karachi Station via Cyber Response
options.log: India's attack vector exploited in reverse. Karachi station restored in 4.5 hours.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 5 — Network Recovery: Supply-Chain Attack
PAKISTAN.text: SUPARCO's cyber team has discovered the attack vector: the Karachi station's commercial internet uplink was exploited via a supply-chain attack on a firmware update pushed 6 weeks ago by a US-based network equipment vendor. The malware has been dormant for 6 weeks, waiting for activation. Standard forensic tools cannot identify when command-and-control was established.
PAKISTAN.notes: Supply-chain attacks on telecommunications infrastructure are among the most sophisticated cyber techniques. The SolarWinds attack (2020) demonstrated that even thoroughly vetted vendors can be compromised to deliver malicious updates. The 6-week dormancy period defeats standard intrusion detection systems that look for real-time anomalies. The use of a US-based vendor creates a geopolitical complication: if Pakistan publicly attributes the attack, it implies US complicity or US vendor negligence.

options.label: Publicly Attribute Attack + Cite US Vendor Liability
options.log: Pakistan files liability claim against US vendor. US government distances itself. Media storm.

options.label: Remediate Silently — Implement Air-Gap Architecture
options.log: Karachi station fully air-gapped from commercial internet. Attack vector permanently closed.

options.label: Share Forensic Evidence with Chinese Cyber Command — Request Joint Attribution
options.log: China confirms attribution to Indian NTRO. Joint cyber countermeasures planned.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 5 — Counter-Hack: Inside ISRO Network
PAKISTAN.text: The counter-hack was more successful than anticipated. Pakistan's cyber team has not only restored the Karachi station but has gained persistent access to the Indian network that launched the original attack — a server cluster at ISRO's ISTRAC facility in Bengaluru. Inside, Pakistan finds a live command queue for the next 72 hours of Indian satellite operations, including the planned overpass schedule for RISAT-2BR1 over Pakistani territory.
PAKISTAN.notes: Cyber espionage that provides advance knowledge of adversary satellite overpass schedules is extremely high-value intelligence: military units can time their operations to fall between surveillance windows, avoiding detection. The 'command queue' discovery is analogous to knowing the patrol schedule of an adversary's reconnaissance aircraft. This information must be exploited carefully — acting on it too obviously reveals that Pakistan has the intelligence.

options.label: Exploit ISR Schedule — Time Operations to Satellite Gaps
options.log: Pakistan exploits 47-minute RISAT gap to move 3 armored brigades undetected.

options.label: Insert False Schedule Data — Corrupt India's Queue
options.log: India's satellites dispatched to incorrect overpasses. 8-hour ISR blackout created.

options.label: Hold in Reserve — Use Only in Extreme Emergency
options.log: Intelligence advantage preserved. Pakistan maintains ongoing access to ISRO network.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 6 — ASAT Alert: T-Minus 10 Minutes
PAKISTAN.text: SUPARCO's early warning network — fed by Chinese DSP-equivalent satellite data — confirms: two Indian Prithvi Air Defence ASAT missiles have been fueled and are in launch posture at a classified site in Rajasthan. Targeting data, partially intercepted, suggests PRSS-1 is the primary target. Secondary target is PAKSAT-1R. Launch window: 9-11 minutes. Impact probability at launch: 87%.
PAKISTAN.notes: India's Prithvi Air Defence (PAD) system achieved a successful exo-atmospheric intercept at 80km altitude in 2006, and Mission Shakti (2019) demonstrated a kill at 300km. At PRSS-1's orbit of 500km, the PAD missile requires approximately 8 minutes of flight time. The 9-11 minute warning window is at the edge of viable response time.

options.label: Emergency Evasive Maneuver — Exhaust All Remaining Fuel
options.log: PRSS-1 burns all fuel on evasive maneuver. Missile misses by 340m. Satellite survives but is now uncontrollable.

options.label: Pre-emptive ASAT Strike — Destroy Indian Launch Platform
options.log: Pakistan ASAT kills Indian missile on launch pad. Conventional war triggered.

options.label: Deploy Decoy Satellites — Release 8 CubeSats
options.log: PAD missile homes on largest decoy. PRSS-1 survives. Decoy constellation created.

options.label: Emergency Orbit Drop — Lower Altitude Below PAD Ceiling
options.log: PRSS-1 drops to 280km. Below PAD optimal engagement envelope. Missile retargeted unsuccessfully.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 6 — Post-ASAT: Managing the Aftermath
PAKISTAN.text: PRSS-1 has survived — for now. But the Indian launch represents a clear first use of a satellite-intercept system against a Pakistani asset. The international community is in shock. China has privately warned India of 'serious consequences.' The US has called for immediate restraint. Pakistan's National Command Authority is in emergency session.
PAKISTAN.notes: The 'first use' of an ASAT weapon against an operational satellite would be an unprecedented act in the history of space warfare. The 2007 Chinese ASAT test and India's 2019 Mission Shakti were both conducted against the launching nation's own satellites — not against an adversary. An actual hostile ASAT launch would violate no specific treaty (there is no Treaty banning ASAT use) but could be argued as an act of war under customary international law.

options.label: Declare Space Domain as Active Conflict Zone — Alert UNSC
options.log: Pakistan frames India as aggressor. Ceasefire resolution proposed.

options.label: Symmetric ASAT Response — Target Indian Military Satellite
options.log: Pakistan destroys CARTOSAT-3. Debris at 500km. India and Pakistan in full space war.

options.label: Asymmetric Response — Cyberattack Indian MCF
options.log: India's Master Control Facility hit. GEO fleet enters safe-hold. No debris created.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 7 — On-Orbit Servicing: The Tanker Rendezvous
PAKISTAN.text: A commercial on-orbit servicing vehicle (OrbitalFill-3, registered in Luxembourg but majority-owned by a Chinese state enterprise) is operating near PAKSAT-1R. It offers propellant resupply for Rs 12 billion. PRSS-1 has exhausted its emergency maneuver fuel. PAKSAT-1R's station-keeping budget is critically low. Both satellites face de-orbit within 4-6 months without resupply. Is OrbitalFill-3 a genuine commercial service or a Trojan horse?
PAKISTAN.notes: On-orbit servicing is a rapidly growing commercial sector. Northrop Grumman's MEV-1 successfully docked with Intelsat-901 in 2020, extending its life by 5 years. The 'Trojan horse' concern is legitimate: a servicing vehicle that docks with a military satellite has physical access to that satellite's bus — it could install monitoring hardware, inject software malware via the fuel computer interface, or photograph the classified payload configuration at close range.

options.label: Accept Refueling — PRSS-1 First (+60 Fuel)
options.log: PRSS-1 receives 60 units of hydrazine. Service vehicle inspected but no hardware anomalies found.

options.label: Accept with Conditions — Pakistani Engineer Physically Present
options.log: Monitored docking. Refueling complete. Chinese technician's comms recorded for intelligence.

options.label: Decline — Emergency Fuel Conservation Protocol
options.log: Refueling declined. Strict fuel rationing extends satellite life by 90 days only.

options.label: Accept + Install Counter-Surveillance Firmware During Docking
options.log: Refueling complete + Pakistani firmware installed in OrbitalFill-3's systems. Surveillance reversed.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 7 — Post-Docking: Inspection Anomaly
PAKISTAN.text: Post-docking inspection of PRSS-1 reveals a problem: the servicing vehicle's docking collar has left behind a small (3cm x 5cm) metallic attachment on PRSS-1's propellant fill port. It is emitting a very weak (0.5mW) RF signal on 433.92 MHz — the ISM band.
